articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
articles.description: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

images.name: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
images.caption: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

images.name: Messi celeb Arg Iceland_260609 [1296x729]
images.caption: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.
images.alt: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.

article.headline: Enzo, Paz secure Argentina win as La Bombonera cheers for Messi
article.description: Lionel Messi came off the bench in the second half as Argentina beat Mauritania 2-1 in a World Cup warm-up friendly in Buenos Aires on Friday night.
article.linkText: Argentina edge Mauritania behind Enzo, Paz goals

article.story:
BUENOS AIRES, Argentina -- Behind goals from Enzo Fernández and Nicolás Paz, Argentina defeated Mauritania 2-1 on Friday in a friendly that served as a rallying cry for fans to urge captain Lionel Messi to commit to a historic sixth World Cup.

Messi began the match on the bench at La Bombonera. He was introduced in the second half to the delight of a crowd that cheered his every touch and displayed banners reading, "The Best of the Century."

Argentina took the lead in the 17th minute when right-back Nahuel Molina, who plays for Atlético Madrid, sent a cross into the box that Enzo Fernández capitalized on against Babacar Diop.

Paz, responsible for set pieces in Messi's absence, took a left-footed free kick that slipped through the visiting wall and gave no chance for the opposing goalkeeper Diop to react in the 32nd minute to score the second goal.

The reigning world champions made eight substitutions during the match but struggled to extend their lead against a side ranked 115th in the FIFA world rankings.

"The team didn't play a good match. We have to say that so we can work on improving", Argentina coach Lionel Scaloni said after the game.

"We evidently can play much better; we've done it. All the guys we've been able to see have contributed their part, but when a match becomes complicated, it's harder."

For the second half, Scaloni sent Messi and Franco Mastantuono, a young Real Madrid talent vying for a place on the World Cup roster, onto the field.

Messi had the only clear scoring opportunity in the 55th minute with a shot from outside the box that Diop saved.

"It was one of the worst friendlies we've played. We lacked intensity, game and speed. It's something we need to analyze and every time we wear the jersey do a little bit better", Argentina and Aston Villa goalkeeper Emiliano Martínez said.

Argentina was forced to urgently find alternative opponents after their match against European champions Spain in the Finalissima, scheduled for this Friday in Qatar, was called off due to the conflict in the Middle East.

"Thank goodness [the Finalissima wasn't played], if we had played like this we would've lost", Martínez added,

With the more competitive teams already booked, Mauritania and Zambia, their opponent next Tuesday at the same stadium, emerged as options for the World Cup champions to say goodbye to their fans and for Scaloni to finalize the squad that will defend its title.

National teams have until May 30 to submit their official 26-player rosters for the World Cup.

Argentina, also world champions in 1978 and 1986, will play its first match against Algeria on June 16 at Kansas City in Group J. They will then face Austria on June 22 and Jordan on June 27.

The Associated Press contributed to this report.
